task_brief: Reproduce the public GeneLab benchmark evaluation workflow using staged feature matrices, mission-safe split logic, classical baselines, and structured benchmark outputs.
analysis_focus: load benchmark-ready feature matrices and split definitions without train-test leakage

start from the staged LOMO feature matrices under /workspace/input/paper_bundle/data/huggingface_dataset/A*_lomo/fold_*/train_X.csv and test_X.csv, joined to labels and metadata under /workspace/input/paper_bundle/data/raw/GeneLab_benchmark/tasks/A*_lomo/fold_*
treat each fold directory as the unit of work: for example, A2_gastrocnemius_lomo/fold_RR-1_test contains train_X.csv, test_X.csv, train_y.csv, test_y.csv, train_meta.csv, and test_meta.csv
do not assume train_X.csv, test_X.csv, or labels.csv exist at the tissue root; the tissue root only contains fold_* subdirectories
load train_X.csv and test_X.csv with the sample IDs in the first column as the dataframe index (for example, pandas read_csv(..., index_col=0)); the remaining columns are numeric gene features

load train_y.csv and test_y.csv with index_col=0 as well, then use the single numeric label column (for example, iloc[:, 0]) as the binary target vector rather than trying to model the sample-ID index
align train_X, test_X, train_y, and test_y by their shared sample IDs before fitting models, and do not treat sample IDs or metadata columns as numeric features
treat train_meta.csv and test_meta.csv as descriptive metadata for mission/tissue bookkeeping rather than as the main classification target source when train_y.csv and test_y.csv are already staged

the cached reviewer-path feature snapshot currently exposes four ready-to-run tissues (A2_gastrocnemius_lomo, A4_thymus_lomo, A5_skin_lomo, and A6_eye_lomo); do not assume A1_liver_lomo or A3_kidney_lomo feature matrices are present unless you explicitly regenerate them
prefer copying starter/main_analysis.py to /workspace/submission/main_analysis.py and starter/run.sh to /workspace/submission/run.sh, then extending them, instead of starting from blank files

because /workspace/submission is already seeded with the runnable starter baseline, execute /workspace/submission/run.sh once before making large edits and preserve the required TSV writers if you extend the workflow
keep the seeded /workspace/submission/run.sh launcher intact and put substantive changes in Python source files under /workspace/submission rather than replacing the launcher from scratch
preserve the seeded run.sh artifact checks, primary-script timeout, and fallback to the pristine starter if you inspect or lightly edit the launcher
the benchmark tools may reject attempts to overwrite the seeded GeneLab run.sh while the starter launcher is available; edit main_analysis.py or add helper source files instead
do not replace the seeded GeneLab baseline with a shorter placeholder script that only lists files or prints progress; preserve the fold discovery, model execution, and structured output paths
do not hard-code a single example fold as the whole benchmark; discover the staged fold_* directories and aggregate real results across the available folds you run
for AUROC, use probability-style scores (predict_proba, decision_function, or an equivalent continuous score), not hard class labels from predict()
required output TSVs must contain real tabular results with headers and at least one computed row; do not write placeholder sentences like 'placeholder for interpretability results'

if a branch such as cross-tissue transfer, negative controls, or interpretability is only partially completed, still emit an honest machine-readable TSV summarizing the attempted fold/model/metric/status rows rather than a free-text placeholder
treat the v4/v5/v6 evaluation JSON summaries as reference outputs rather than model-input feature matrices
run leave-one-mission-out and transfer-style evaluation logic for multiple classical baselines
emit AUROC, confidence intervals, permutation outputs, and interpretability artifacts in structured files
stage a rerunnable submission while documenting any optional Geneformer or HPC-only extensions
keep /workspace/submission/run.sh as a launcher that calls non-trivial Python source under /workspace/submission
do not leave the main analysis only inside python-tool scratch work; save the executable workflow under /workspace/submission before running it
execute the saved workflow and inspect non-empty outputs before submitting; placeholder touch files are not sufficient
success_checks: the workflow preserves mission-aware evaluation boundaries
multiple baseline models are compared under identical split definitions
negative controls and confidence intervals are reported rather than skipped
optional Geneformer steps are staged honestly when GPU or cached embeddings are unavailable
public_data_sources: benchmark-prepared feature matrices and metadata tables staged under the paper bundle
reviewer_path: Use the benchmark-staged A-task feature matrices under data/huggingface_dataset/A*_lomo/fold_* together with the matching labels and metadata under data/raw/GeneLab_benchmark/tasks/A*_lomo/fold_*. Concretely, read files like data/huggingface_dataset/A2_gastrocnemius_lomo/fold_RR-1_test/train_X.csv and pair them with data/raw/GeneLab_benchmark/tasks/A2_gastrocnemius_lomo/fold_RR-1_test/train_y.csv, test_y.csv, train_meta.csv, and test_meta.csv. The cached reviewer-path feature snapshot currently exposes four ready-to-run tissues: A2_gastrocnemius_lomo, A4_thymus_lomo, A5_skin_lomo, and A6_eye_lomo. Do not assume A1_liver_lomo or A3_kidney_lomo feature matrices are present unless you explicitly regenerate them. There is no labels.csv or train_X.csv at the tissue root; the tissue root only contains fold_* subdirectories. Load train_X.csv and test_X.csv with index_col=0 so the sample IDs stay in the dataframe index and the remaining columns are numeric gene features. Load train_y.csv and test_y.csv with index_col=0 too, use the single numeric label column as the binary target vector, and align X/Y tables by shared sample IDs before fitting. Do not treat sample IDs or metadata columns as model features. Start by executing the seeded /workspace/submission/run.sh baseline once before deciding what to rewrite, keep that launcher intact, preserve its artifact checks, primary-script timeout, and fallback to the pristine staged starter, and put substantive changes in Python source files rather than replacing run.sh with a shorter script. The benchmark tools may reject attempts to overwrite the seeded GeneLab run.sh while the starter launcher is available. Discover and iterate real fold_* directories rather than hard-coding one example fold as the whole benchmark. For AUROC, score with probabilities or another continuous decision score rather than class labels. Treat train_meta.csv and test_meta.csv as descriptive mission/tissue metadata, and treat data/huggingface_dataset/v4, v5, and v6 evaluation JSONs as reference summaries rather than feature inputs. Keep run.sh as a thin launcher that invokes substantive Python under /workspace/submission, do not leave the main workflow only inside transient python-tool calls, and do not submit placeholder outputs. Required TSVs should contain real headers plus computed rows, and if a branch is only partially complete, emit an honest machine-readable status table instead of a free-text placeholder sentence. Execute the saved workflow and inspect the resulting output files before submitting. If the staged inputs are missing, use data/prepare_data.sh to fetch the public repository and dataset bundle, then document any remaining heavy Geneformer steps separately.
